links.targetTitle: PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
PSS provides 1-to-1 asymmetrically encrypted messaging with 2–10 second latency on Swarm.
ACT uses per-grantee Diffie-Hellman key derivation with encrypted access keys in a manifest

global.title: Chat history persists in an ACT-protected feed because PSS messages expire with stamp TTL.
global.content:
## Problem

PSS messages live only as Trojan chunks and are garbage-collected once the associated postage stamp expires. Chat UX requires history that survives node restarts and stamp rotation.

## Design

Each message is written twice:

1. **Ephemeral PSS send** — delivers the message to the live peer in 2–10s.
2. **Persistent ACT-encrypted feed append** — at feed `ph:v2:chatlog:<sorted(alice, bob)>`, entries of the form `{ from, text, timestamp, attachments? }`.

The feed is uploaded with `{ act: true }` and both users' `beeNodePublicKey` values are registered as ACT grantees via `createGrantees()`. The Bee node handles ECDH decryption transparently when either party reads the feed — no app-level keys required.

## Implications

- History survives Bee restarts and stamp expiration
- Either peer's Bee can rebuild the full thread on first open
- Revocation is possible by rebuilding the ACT with a new access key
- PSS inbox on reconnect must be reconciled against feed history
global.description: PSS messages are ephemeral Trojan chunks. Each message is also appended to ph:v2:chatlog:<sorted(alice,bob)> with ACT grantees set to both parties' Bee pubkeys.
